assemblyInfo.submitter: OKINAWA INSTITUTE OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY

bioprojects.title: Telomere-to-telomere assembly of the genome of an individual Oikopleura dioica from Okinawa using Nanopore-based sequencing

description.title: I69
description.comment: High-molecular weight DNA from a male individual (ID: I69) of the Okinawan Oikopleura dioica. The individual was collected from on-doing laboratory culture deriving from the wild population in Ishikawa Harbor, Okinawa. Around 200 ng of the DNA was sequenced with Oxford Nanopore MinION technology. In addition, around 5 ng of the I69 genomic DNA was whole-genome amplified and sequenced with Illumina MiSeq platform. The long-read Nanopore and short-read Illumina sequencing data from this individual was used to produce the I69-5 genome assembly.

assemblyInfo.comments: The OKI2018_I69 assembly has a total length of 64.3 Mbp distributed among 19 scaffolds. 99% of the assembly is contained within five megabase-scale scaffolds. First two scaffolds correspond to autosomal chromosomes, chr1 and chr2. The sex chromosomes are split into pseudo-autosomal region (PAR) and X-specific region (XSR) or Y-specific region (YSR). chrUn_12 is a draft assembly of mitochondrial genome.
